name: Climb to the Santuario de Arrate and Busturialdea roads - circular from Eibar
name_original: Subida al Santuario de Arrate y carreteras de Busturialdea — circular desde Eibar
region_intro: Road cycling around Ermua is characterized by rugged natural features and challenging terrain, situated in the western part of the Duranguesado region. The landscape features steep foothills and profound valleys, protected by the Urko and Ureta mountains. This varied topography provides opportunities for challenging climbs and diverse road cycling routes. The region is known for its excellent road surfaces, making it suitable for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Ermua The most popular road cycling route is Climb to the Santuario de Arrate and Busturialdea roads - circular from Eibar, a 51.4 miles (82.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 58 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain, including the iconic Alto de Arrate climb. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Areitio Climb – View of Anboto from Atxondo loop from Ermua, a moderate 29.2 miles (46.9 km) path. This route includes a notable climb and offers views towards Anboto, showcasing varied and engaging terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Altzola Church – Panoramic view of Deba loop from Ermua, a 35.4 miles (57.0 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 43 minutes. Road cycling around Ermua is defined by rugged natural features, steep foothills, and profound valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate climbs to difficult, long-distance routes. The routes in Ermua are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 7000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Ermua's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Ermua?
A: Ermua offers a diverse selection of over 10 road cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find everything from moderate climbs to challenging, long-distance rides.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Ermua?
A: The terrain around Ermua is characterized by its rugged nature, featuring beautiful valleys nestled between steep hillsides. You can expect challenging climbs and descents, with routes often winding through the narrow Ego River valley and offering dramatic backdrops from the Urko and Ureta mountains. Most routes are on paved surfaces.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Ermua?
A: While specific weather data isn't provided, the varied topography suggests that spring and autumn would likely offer the most pleasant temperatures for tackling the challenging climbs. Summer can be warm, and winter might bring colder, potentially wetter conditions in the mountains.

Q: Are there options for different fitness levels, including beginners?
A: Yes, Ermua offers routes for various fitness levels. While many routes feature significant elevation gain and are rated moderate to difficult, there are also options suitable for those looking for a less strenuous ride. For example, you can find routes with moderate climbs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20543252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Areitio Climb – View of Anboto from Atxondo loop from Ermua&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What scenic viewpoints or landmarks can I find along road cycling routes in Ermua?
A: Road cycling routes around Ermua often pass through scenic areas. You might encounter natural monuments and peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Udalaitz Peak (1,119 m)&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Kalamua Summit&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4056489" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Miguel de Arretxinaga&lt;/a&gt;. The region's topography itself provides many panoramic views.

Q: Are there any specific climbs known for their views?
A: Yes, the region is known for its challenging climbs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20543252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Areitio Climb – View of Anboto from Atxondo loop from Ermua&lt;/a&gt; offers views towards Anboto. Other notable climbs mentioned in the region include Trabakua Pass and Alto de Ixua, which are part of routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20623384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Migel / Urkaregi – Alto de Ixua loop from Ermua&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes from Ermua?
A: For those seeking longer rides, Ermua offers several extensive routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39566137" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panoramic view of Deba – Zarautz Beach loop from Ermua&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, covers over 100 km and includes coastal sections, typically taking around 5 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any loop routes for road cycling in Ermua?
A: Many of the road cycling routes around Ermua are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33984869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Altzola Church – Panoramic view of Deba loop from Ermua&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20623384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Migel / Urkaregi – Alto de Ixua loop from Ermua&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Ermua?
A: The road cycling routes in Ermua are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 8,000 road cyclists have explored the varied terrain, often praising the challenging climbs and scenic beauty.

Q: Is public transport accessible for cyclists in Ermua?
A: Ermua's strategic location within the Duranguesado region suggests that public transport options might connect it to larger towns, potentially offering access for cyclists. However, specific information regarding bike-friendly public transport services would require checking local transport provider details.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Ermua?
A: As a town that serves as a hub for outdoor activities, Ermua likely has public parking facilities available. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the starting points for designated parking areas or general town parking options.

Q: Are there any routes that offer coastal views?
A: Yes, some routes from Ermua extend towards the coast, offering stunning sea views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39566137" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panoramic view of Deba – Zarautz Beach loop from Ermua&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, leading through coastal areas and providing expansive vistas of the Bay of Biscay.
tips: Quiet climb along a very blue lake with some very steep pinches. The road quality is okay but I wouldn't recommend it as a descent
Climb that is not very long but that is famous for the World Tour peloton, in the return that is done every year in the Basque Country
The Gontzegaraigane is 2.6 km long, has its highest point at 370 m and rises over a distance of about 2.6 km. The average slope of Gontzegaraigane is 6.5%.
Quite steep last part of the climb and very steep last part of the descent (working brakes recommended)

The Alto de Ixua is an iconic mountain pass in Eibar, with steep slopes that make it a classic Basque cycling route. From its summit, you can enjoy sweeping views of the mountains of Gipuzkoa and the industrial valley of Eibar.
The road leads you to the legendary Arrate Sanctuary, a historic summit for Basque cycling and the final stage of numerous editions of the Tour of the Basque Country and the Tour of Spain.
